run_summary: Screen 1 faces 10 new flags from two catalysts (OpenAI voice models and Claude Cowork expansion). Seven flags pass BUY eligibility; four qualify under the basket rule (same Claude Cowork trigger), requiring exploratory tier. Two conviction-tier opportunities exist (ONTO, AEIS, PLAB via separate trigger analysis). Position actions focus on end-of-holding-window decisions for 14-day positions.

entries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: OpenAI's voice model release has no plausible mechanism to displace donor-management workflows, integrated payments, or peer-to-peer fundraising platforms. Blackbaud's own Risk Factors explicitly warn that stock price may decline on AI market perception 'regardless of our actual operating performance' — this is exactly the pattern we're seeing today.
entries.what_kills: A subsequent 8-K disclosing customer churn, a security incident update, or renewal-cohort weakness in the ~40% of contracts up for renewal in 2026.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OpenAI voice catalyst, 5 flags total from this trigger (BLKB, NCNO, PAYC, PCTY, DUOL) — basket rule applies. Confidence 4, threat_assessment 'minimal', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Filings confirm Risk Factors explicitly warn 'stock price may decline on AI market perception regardless of operating performance' — precisely the sympathy fade pattern. Donor-management SaaS with 3-year subscription lock and embedded payments has zero voice-AI displacement risk. Assigned exploratory tier per basket rule (not conviction, despite strong filings read).

red_team.weakest_link: The flag assumes a rapid mean-reversion bounce despite being classified as 'OVERDONE' with a -5.01% one-day move and only 0.58x volume, which are not yet sufficient to confirm capitulation.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis rests on the premise that this is a pure sympathy fade with no fundamental risk, supported by Risk Factor language that is boilerplate and not specific to voice AI. However, the five-day change of -3.34% shows the selloff has persisted beyond a single-day dip, and volume of 0.58x (below average) combined with a -5.01% move suggests the market may be repricing on broader concerns—not just AI headlines. Blackbaud has a history of security issues; the thesis does not adequately address whether this move is conflating recent memory of those events with the current AI catalyst.

entries.name: nCino, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: OpenAI's voice model release does not threaten bank loan origination or onboarding workflows — these require regulatory audit trails, KYC/AML compliance, and integration with core banking systems that a voice agent cannot replicate. Move is thematic AI-SaaS aversion, not substantive.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that a major FI customer is piloting a competing LLM-based loan origination platform, or a downgrade tied to AI displacement risk.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OpenAI voice catalyst, part of 5-flag basket from same trigger. Confidence 4, threat 'minimal', panic 'unjustified'. Filings note AI/ML as competitive risk, but customer base is regulated FIs (OCC/FDIC) with 3-5 year contracts on Salesforce Platform. Voice agents cannot replicate KYC/AML/audit-trail requirements for loan origination. Thematic panic, zero mechanism. Exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The rebound logic ('five-day change is still positive') is contradicted by the fact that a -3.99% one-day move occurred despite the five-day backdrop, indicating either a sharp intraday reversal or renewed selling pressure today.
red_team.critique: The five-day change of +6.06% does support the mean-reversion read, but it masks a -3.99% single-day decline today, suggesting the market is re-evaluating the thesis even within a positive 5-day context. The thesis correctly identifies zero displacement risk for KYC/AML workflows, but does not address whether FI customers are simply pausing spending in anticipation of AI disruption elsewhere in their tech stacks, creating indirect headwinds independent of nCino's actual product moat.

entries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: Voice models don't file 941s, run payroll ACH, or handle multi-state UI compliance. Paycom's moat is regulatory infrastructure and being the bank-of-record for employee comp — a text/voice interface doesn't displace that. Five-day change is still strongly positive, so this is a single-day sympathy dip.
entries.what_kills: An unusual client-loss disclosure or evidence that agentic AI is being adopted for payroll compliance in a regulated context.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OpenAI voice catalyst, part of 5-flag basket. Confidence 4, threat 'minimal', panic 'unjustified'. Payroll is regulatory (941s, ACH, multi-state UI compliance). Five-day change still +10.2% (strongly positive), confirming single-day sympathy dip. Voice models don't file taxes. Exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull thesis leans heavily on the five-day +10.2% return to dismiss today's -3.67% move as a low-conviction dip, but does not account for the possibility that the strong 5-day move was driven by earnings/guidance tailwinds that are now being partially reversed by the AI catalyst.
red_team.critique: While the regulatory-moat argument is sound, the five-day context is ambiguous: a +10.2% move followed by a -3.67% reversal could signal that the prior strength was event-driven and the current slide is a correction, rather than a pure thematic fade. The low volume (0.47x) is consistent with low conviction, but it could also indicate weak hands exiting a position that rose too fast, leaving the stock vulnerable to further weakness if sentiment shifts.

entries.name: Paylocity Holding Corporation
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: Same thesis as PAYC — voice models don't handle payroll tax filings, ACH banking relationships, or state-by-state employment law compliance. The five-day change is still strongly positive, confirming this is a one-day thematic dip on the OpenAI headline.
entries.what_kills: A negative preannouncement or evidence that agentic AI is displacing HR SaaS in Paylocity's SMB/mid-market segment.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OpenAI voice catalyst, part of 5-flag basket. Confidence 4, threat 'minimal', panic 'unjustified'. Same thesis as PAYC—payroll tax filing, ACH banking, multi-jurisdictional compliance moat is untouched by voice AI. Five-day +10.22% confirms thematic dip. Exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis assumes PAYC/PCTY co-movement is evidence of thematic fade, but a -3.05% move in PCTY alongside -3.67% in PAYC is too small a sample to confirm sympathy rather than name-specific selling.
red_team.critique: The regulatory-moat thesis is defensible, but the basket-rule framing (five flags from one trigger) creates confirmation bias: the thesis assumes co-movement proves sympathy fade, when it could instead reflect differentiated weakness in payroll/HR SaaS relative to other verticals. The five-day +10.22% is supportive of mean-reversion, but without clarity on what drove that 5-day gain, it is difficult to argue the current -3.05% is truly exogenous to Paylocity's own positioning.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: OpenAI's real-time voice conversation capability is more directly relevant to Duolingo than to most names in this basket — but Duolingo already offers AI-powered voice conversation practice (Duolingo Max), and the core moat is the streak/gamification dopamine loop, not instructional depth. The five-day change is still strongly positive, and today's move is a modest thematic dip, not a real repricing.
entries.what_kills: MAU/DAU deceleration data or evidence that Duolingo Max subscriber growth is stalling as users shift to native ChatGPT voice for language practice — this would elevate to 'partial' or 'justified'.
entries.reasoning: OpenAI voice catalyst, part of 5-flag basket. Confidence 3 (below 4). Threat_assessment is 'indirect' (not 'minimal' or 'none'). Filings acknowledge explicit GenAI competitive risk. Duolingo already has Duolingo Max (AI-powered voice practice), and core moat is gamification/streak dopamine loop, not content. Five-day change +10.91% is still positive. The threat read is more credible than the other basket members, but confidence is threshold-adjacent (3) and threat is indirect. WATCH for one more trading day to see if voice-adoption data emerges; if DUOL remains up 5%+ and no user-engagement cracks appear, can elevate to BUY exploratory on next refresh.

entries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: Voice AI has no plausible application to healthcare provider analytics/data warehousing. However, HCAT has real company-specific issues (Project Nexus restructuring, CEO transition, DOS-to-Ignite migration churn) that make it hard to isolate the sympathy-fade signal from operational headwinds. Modest confidence and small position size warranted.
entries.what_kills: Any incremental negative on Project Nexus execution, DOS migration churn, or a downgrade — HCAT has real issues that could dominate the tape independent of AI sentiment.
entries.reasoning: Confidence 2 (below min_buy_confidence threshold of 3). Does not meet BUY eligibility bar.

entries.name: Cognex Corporation
entries.catalyst: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.thesis: Cognex has real, filings-acknowledged AI competition — but it's from open-source machine-vision models on factory floors, NOT from a coding agent for office productivity. Claude Cowork does not do deterministic sub-millisecond machine vision on a QA line. The panic is thematic, applying a general 'AI eats software' narrative to a very specific factory-floor workflow. The filings acknowledge the general threat but the trigger event itself does nothing to accelerate it.
entries.what_kills: A major factory-automation customer (auto OEM, EV battery, life sciences) publicly switching to a general-purpose AI vision solution; or an open-source model demonstrating deterministic latency parity in an industrial-grade setting.
entries.reasoning: Claude Cowork catalyst. Confidence 3, threat_assessment 'indirect' (filings explicitly acknowledge AI competition in machine vision, but Claude Cowork is a productivity/coding agent, not a factory-floor vision system). Deterministic sub-millisecond QA-line vision has no overlap with office coding agents. Five-day change -6.08% is modest. Threat read is credible but applied to the wrong trigger event. Confidence at threshold. WATCH for 1-2 days; if CGNX stabilizes and no factory-automation customer-loss news emerges, can escalate to exploratory BUY.
